title: Idempotent Consumers, Inbox Records, and External Effects · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A messaging pairing room predicts publisher, exchange, queue, consumer, ownership, and failure transitions before assembling the first durable inbox and duplicate-safe effect protocol for idempotent consumers, inbox records, and external effects. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of durable inbox and duplicate-safe effect protocol while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Derive a stable scoped operation identity from producer intent, tenant, message type, and semantic version rather than delivery metadata.
Record inbox identity, input fingerprint, state transition, domain effect, and completion atomically in one database transaction where possible.
Reject reuse of an operation identity with a different payload fingerprint or semantic intent and retain conflict evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Stable Idempotency Key
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop messaging team handles case 7390 in a recovery drill with stale callbacks from an old channel generation. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reject fingerprint conflicts, suppress completed duplicates, and preserve external ambiguity; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive a stable scoped operation identity from producer intent, tenant, message type, and semantic version rather than delivery metadata.

What must be established before implementation?
